new_classes.label_en: Wine with Protected Designation of Origin (PDO) in containers ≤ 2 L (subheading 2204 21 68)
new_classes.label_de: Wein mit geschützter Ursprungsbezeichnung (g.U.) in Behältnissen mit einem Inhalt von 2 l oder weniger (Unterposition 2204 21 68)
new_classes.definition_en: Still wine of fresh grapes bearing a Protected Designation of Origin (PDO) as defined in Additional Note 6(a) to Chapter 22, presented in containers holding 2 litres or less. This subheading covers PDO wines within the range 2204 21 11 to 2204 21 78.

coverage_explanation:
The two primary distinguishing criteria are captured: (1) the wine bears a Protected Designation of Origin (PDO), modelled via the existing ProtectedDesignationOfOriginOrGeographicalIndicationQuality quality class; (2) it is presented in containers ≤ 2 L, modelled via the existing SmallContainer class using the BFO has-part relation. 

Gaps that reduce the score:
- The legal note cross-references "Additional Note 6(a) to Chapter 22" for the precise definition of PDO wine; this note is not available in the current TBox context, so the full scope of PDO criteria (e.g. specific geographic areas, production rules, alcohol content sub-ranges) cannot be axiomatised. The ProtectedDesignationOfOriginOrGeographicalIndicationQuality quality class covers PDO generically but does not distinguish PDO from PGI, which Additional Note 6(a) may do more precisely.
- The subheading sits within the range 2204 21 11–78, implying there are sibling subheadings that further subdivide PDO wines (e.g. by alcohol content or colour). Without the full text of Additional Note 6(a) and the sibling subheading legal texts, the specific discriminating alcohol-by-volume thresholds or wine-type distinctions for code 22042168 cannot be fully encoded.
